name: marigold-http-patch
status: PENDING deploy of POST /api/docs/:id/patch (branch feat/patch-ops). Identical to marigold-http.json EXCEPT the UPDATE phase, which POSTs a small ops patch instead of a full-page PUT. Run locally against the dev server (--base-url http://localhost:3100) at --size small — the ops below are calibrated to small.html's structural marigoldIds. Compare its UPDATE wire median against targets/marigold-http.json (PUT full-replace) at the same size/base-url: that is the patch-vs-fullreplace latency measurement (see README 'Patch vs full-replace comparison').
notes: Patch variant of marigold-http.json. CREATE / VERIFY1 / VERIFY2 / FEEDBACK are byte-for-byte identical; only UPDATE differs.

UPDATE posts a fixed two-op patch that reproduces the small.revision.json change (replace the status cell + append the Update 1 section) while sending only ~300B of ops instead of the whole ~1.4KB page — the whole point of patch ops.

run-http.mjs's generic {content} substitution can't derive structural marigoldIds from a fixture, so the ops are hardcoded. They are calibrated for tasks/small.html: replace targets the `td[data-bench=rev-target]` cell (mg-be9768b01c), append targets the footer `p.footer` (mg-e675ff0e10). Recompute per size with the one-liner in README before running --size medium|large (ids are structural: derive them by instrumenting the fixture).
The runner prints a PENDING warning for this target — that's expected until /api/docs/:id/patch is deployed to prod; until then run with --base-url http://localhost:3100.

Promotion criterion (see README): patch ops promote if UPDATE wall-clock improves >=2x vs full-content update at >=30KB docs. Quote this target's result JSON next to marigold-http.json's at the same size.

ops.html:
Update 1

Revision applied by agent-bench: pricing fact-check complete; figures confirmed against the status table. bench-update-1.

claim.notes: Not part of the benchmark phases; recorded so the descriptor documents the full planned surface.
